title: Incidents do not arrive as clean bug reports.
body: RePro turns screenshots, logs, PDFs, stack traces, and pressure into a verified, approval-gated pull request with readable proof.
narration: Incidents do not arrive as clean bug reports. They arrive as screenshots, logs, PDFs, stack traces, and pressure. RePro is built for that moment, turning messy evidence into a verified, approval-gated pull request with readable proof.

title: The dashboard keeps live work legible.
body: Runs, approvals, confidence, and recent activity stay in one control surface so teams can see what the system is doing and what happens next.
narration: Inside the operator dashboard, active runs, approvals, confidence, and recent activity live together. This is the control plane for teams who need instant clarity on what the agent is doing, what it found, and what happens next.

title: Starting a run is structured, not magical.
body: Operators choose an allowlisted repository, add incident context, and decide whether to stop at dry-run or continue through apply-and-verify.
narration: Starting a run is structured, not magical. The operator selects an allowlisted repository, attaches context, and chooses whether to stop at dry-run or continue through apply-and-verify. Every choice reinforces bounded autonomy instead of unchecked execution.

title: Policy is part of the product.
body: Repository-specific commands, outbound rules, package managers, and CI hooks stay visible and reviewable so the workflow can scale without dropping its safety model.
narration: Recipe policies make the workflow portable. Repository-specific commands, outbound network rules, package managers, and CI hooks stay visible and reviewable. That lets RePro scale across teams while preserving the safety model each repo requires.

title: The trace is the trust layer.
body: Evidence parsing, suspect files, sandbox execution, and verification status update in real time as a readable narrative of what the system actually did.
narration: Once a run begins, RePro opens the trace. Evidence is parsed, suspect files are narrowed, sandbox execution starts, and verification status updates in real time. The trace is the trust layer: readable proof of what the system actually did.

title: The detail page turns verification into evidence.
body: Confidence, reproducibility, observability coverage, replay actions, and trace links sit beside the underlying evidence before approval is requested.
narration: When verification succeeds, the detail page becomes proof. Confidence, reproducibility, observability coverage, replay actions, and trace links sit beside the evidence. The system earns the right to ask for approval because it has already shown its work.

title: Every run becomes operational memory.
body: Run history, approval records, policy inventory, and incident correlation make the control plane durable long after the live fix is done.
narration: Administrators get the full control plane: run history, approval records, policy inventory, and incident correlation. That is where the long-term value shows up. Every run becomes reusable operational memory instead of another debugging session lost to chat.

title: Voice feeds the same governed engine.
body: The operator can start from speech, pick a repository, and hand the work to the same policy-aware execution loop.
narration: And when the operator is away from the keyboard, the voice surface keeps the workflow accessible. Voice is not a gimmick here. It is another intake path into the same governed execution engine.

title: More than a coding agent.
body: Verification-first software reliability with multimodal intake, sandboxed execution, human approval at the edge, and auditable proof at the end.
narration: Put together, RePro is more than a coding agent. It is a verification-first software reliability system: multimodal intake on the front end, sandboxed execution in the middle, human approval at the edge, and auditable proof at the end.
